project_context.target_audience: Female high school athletes, their parents, and college coaches
project_context.visual_mood: Premium, highly athletic, empowering, focused, and distinct from the Men's division. Emphasis on speed, agility, and elite collegiate aesthetics.

colors.strategy: Contrasting with the Men's Dark Slate & Orange, the Women's division uses a richer, deeper Navy (Midnight) paired with a high-energy Electric Rose/Coral to evoke a powerful, modern, and elite WNBA/NCAA aesthetic.

typography.strategy: Avoid the Men's condensed, industrial look (Barlow Condensed). Use a geometric, wide, and highly legible modern sans-serif that breathes and feels premium. No Inter for headings.

headings.description: Modern, clean, slight geometric curve. Gives a high-end tech/athletic feel.

body.description: Excellent readability for data-heavy dashboards, tracks well at small sizes.

layout_and_spacing.strategy: Move away from the Men's tightly packed, grid-locked 'stats only' vibe to a slightly more editorial, spacious layout. It must still function perfectly as a SaaS dashboard, but with better breathing room.
layout_and_spacing.dashboard_grid: Bento-style Control Room Grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4) with generous gap-6 or gap-8.
layout_and_spacing.card_treatment: Softer, premium feel. Use rounded-2xl (vs Men's sharper edges). Use a delicate 1px border (border-stone-200) and a very subtle soft shadow (shadow-[0_8px_30px_rgba(0,0,0,0.04)]).
layout_and_spacing.button_treatment: Pill-shaped (rounded-full) for primary actions to distinctively separate from the Men's section. Include a subtle inner glow on hover.

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Buttons must elevate slightly (-translate-y-[1px]) and increase shadow intensity. Explicitly set hover text colors (e.g., hover:bg-rose-700 hover:text-white).
motion_and_interactions.glassmorphism: Use the 'Crystal Glass' effect (backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/70 with a 1px border-white/40) for sticky headers and modal overlays to feel elevated and clean.

components.sidebar: Full height, Midnight Navy background (bg-[#0a1128]). Use Electric Rose text/indicator lines for the active navigation state, contrasting against the dark background.
components.charts: Use Recharts or Shadcn charts. Use smooth curving lines (monotone) instead of jagged linear lines. Incorporate gradients under the chart lines dropping from Electric Rose to transparent.
components.data_testid_requirements: ALL interactive elements (buttons, links, form inputs, toggles, sport switchers) MUST have a data-testid attribute formatted in kebab-case based on their role (e.g., data-testid='womens-dashboard-cta', data-testid='theme-switcher').

theme_switching_strategy.mechanism: Use a data attribute on the root html or body tag: <html data-sports-division="womens">. In your CSS, scope the Women's variables to this attribute.
theme_switching_strategy.ui_toggle: Provide a premium segmented control or toggle switch in the main navigation or profile dropdown to switch between 'Men\'s Basketball' and 'Women\'s Basketball', instantly updating the data attribute without a page reload.

media_and_images.strategy: Focus on high-quality women's athletic action shots and premium university campus visuals. Tint hero images slightly with a Navy-to-Rose gradient overlay if text is placed on top.

image_urls.description: High-intensity women's basketball action. Use as a background or feature card for the profile/onboarding.
Secondary women's basketball action shot, ideal for the coach contact directory or college browsing headers.
Urban university basketball court, good for college program visual representation or placeholder imagery for universities.

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure you inject the DM Sans and Outfit fonts into the project. Set up the CSS variables scoping using `data-sports-division='womens'`. Implement the bento-grid layouts with generous padding. Apply `rounded-2xl` to cards and `rounded-full` to buttons. Add `data-testid` to all interactive components. Use the provided Unsplash/Pexels images directly—DO NOT use placeholder text or dummy image services.
